assembly_instructions: Skid-steer hydraulic tractor built entirely from bolted square-tube steel. A rectangular modular-beam chassis carries a four-wheel layout driven by hub-mounted hydraulic universal rotors and planetary gear-downs. A rear-mounted PowerCube provides hydraulic power to everything; hoses run along the chassis to a universal quick-coupler. A welded operator space frame sits atop the chassis; at the front, a pivot plate supports a loader arm that lifts a front bucket. A universal tool bar at the rear accepts implements (ripper, backhoe, auger).

info.japanese_name: ライフトラック（オープンソース・トラクター）
info.summary: Bolt-together, open-source, four-wheel hydraulic skid-steer tractor. Foundational workhorse of OSE's 50-machine Global Village Construction Set.
info.description: LifeTrac is a modular, open-source tractor developed by Open Source Ecology as the prime mover of its Global Village Construction Set. The entire machine is built from bolted 4-inch square steel tubing — a 'XYZ space frame' — so that individual modules (wheels, PowerCube, loader arm, tool bar) can be swapped in the field without welding. Each of the four wheels is driven independently by a hydraulic 'universal rotor' hub motor through a planetary gear-down, enabling true skid-steer turning and four-wheel independent traction. Hydraulic power comes from a removable PowerCube engine/pump module mounted on the chassis; flows are distributed via quick-couplers to the loader-arm cylinders, the drive motors, and any attached implement. The front loader arm pivots on a welded pivot plate and carries a standard bucket; the rear universal tool bar accepts rippers, three-point hitches, augers, and backhoes. All CAD, bill of materials, and build instructions are released under CC BY-SA 4.0.
info.operator: Open Source Ecology and community builders (farms, training sites, maker communities)
info.contractor: Open Source Ecology (Marcin Jakubowski et al.)
info.status: Open-source, production-tested; multiple independent builds in service

facts.label: Configuration
facts.value: Four-wheel hydraulic skid-steer, modular bolted XYZ space frame

facts.label: Power
facts.value: Removable PowerCube (diesel or gasoline, ≈27 hp typical)

facts.label: Rear attachment
facts.value: Universal tool bar — ripper, 3-point hitch, backhoe, auger

sources.title: OSE — Global Village Construction Set overview

parts.name: Wheel (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: Independently driven front-left wheel. Reacts drive torque from its own universal-rotor hub motor.

parts.name: Modular-Beam Chassis
parts.role: Bolted 4-inch square-tube chassis that forms the load path between the four wheels and every other module.

parts.name: XYZ Space Frame (Operator Cage)
parts.role: Three-axis bolted tube superstructure above the chassis; houses the operator seat, controls, and ROPS.

parts.name: PowerCube
parts.role: Modular diesel/gas engine + hydraulic pump block. Supplies pressure to every hydraulic consumer on the tractor.

parts.name: Loader Pivot Plate
parts.role: Front-mounted pivot bracket that the loader arm rotates on.

parts.name: Loader Arm
parts.role: Hydraulic-lift boom hinged at the pivot plate; raises and tilts the front bucket.

parts.name: Front Bucket
parts.role: Universal front bucket for earth, gravel, or material handling.

parts.name: Universal Rotor (Hub Motor)
parts.role: Standardized hydraulic hub motor at each wheel. Shown on the front-right wheel as representative of all four.

parts.name: Planetary Gear-Down
parts.role: Reduction gearbox between the universal rotor and the wheel hub. Gives low-speed/high-torque drive.

parts.name: Hydraulic Hose
parts.role: High-pressure hose routing fluid from the PowerCube along the chassis to the quick-couplers and loader cylinders.

parts.name: Universal Quick-Coupler
parts.role: Standard hydraulic quick-disconnect. Lets tools and implements be hot-swapped without draining the system.

parts.name: Universal Tool Bar
parts.role: Rear mounting bar that accepts standard implements (ripper, 3-point hitch, backhoe, auger).
